title: Episode 705: A Real Science That Can Do Magic
segment: Finally the Consulting Occultist profiles Chicago-based mail order woo-purveyor Sydney Flowers.
author: Robin

title: Episode 699: Double Deepfake
segment: Finally the Consulting Occultist fulfills a previous promise to cover Arabic geomancy.
author: Robin

title: Episode 695: You’ve Clearly Never Dealt with Balloon People
segment: Finally the Consulting Occultist gives us the lowdown on frequently arrested Elizabethan magician Simon Forman.
author: Robin

title: Episode 691 – From Such Triangles As
segment: Finally the Consulting Occultist regales you with miracles attributed to Pythagoras.
author: Robin

title: Episode 687: Speaking of Magical Tools
segment: Finally at the behest of beloved Patreon backer Bart Mallio the Consulting Occultist reveals all about the magic of chess.
author: Robin

title: Episode 679: His Frequency is Powerful
segment: Finally the Consulting Occultist fills us in on Douglas MacArthur’s role in Korean shamanism.
author: Robin

title: Episode 674: As I Used to Pronounce It
segment: Finally the Consulting Occultist, at the behest of beloved Patreon backer Jason Thompson, looks at the esoteric side of filmmaker Alejandro Jodorowsky. CW: sexual assault.
author: Robin

title: Episode 668: Skating and Painting
segment: Finally the Consulting Occultist answers a request from estimable backer Michael Maneval for a profile of cosmically illuminated sculptor and painter Walter Russell.
author: Robin

title: Episode 661: Dismiss All the Butlers
segment: Finally the Consulting Occultist fulfills beloved Patreon backer Christopher Hatty’s request for a profile of Theosophist turned Buddhist, opera singer turned suspected anarchist spy, Alexandra David-Néel.
author: Robin

title: Episode 658: Personality Temu
segment: Finally the Consulting Occultist suggests traditions of immortality estimable backer Bob Grider might include in his Yellow King game.
author: Robin

title: Episode 655: You Think Too Much About Pole Arms
segment: Finally backer and sponsor John Kovalic asks the Consulting Occultist about 1960s blues rock musician Graham Bond, who considered himself the son of Aleister Crowley and feuded with rival magicians.
author: Robin

title: Episode 652: Break a Window and Wait for Film Night
segment: Finally the Consulting Occultist obeys the command of estimable backer Justin Mohareb, who wants to know about Kabbalists who used their powers against fascism.
author: Robin

title: Episode 648: Hey Isn’t That Ezra Pound?
segment: Finally the Consulting Occultist tells the tale of Florence Farr, an actor and Golden Dawn member whose artistic life intertwined with those of Shaw, Yeats, and Pound.
author: Robin

title: Episode 643: Not Even Enough Gnomes
segment: Finally the Consulting Occultist reveals the career of Albin Grau, ritual magician and production designer for the 1922 Nosferatu.
author: Robin

title: Episode 639: This Appendix Should Not Have Been Printed
segment: Finally, at the behest of estimable Patreon backer Steve Dempsey, the Consulting Occultist fulfills its Canadian content requirements with a look at William Lyon Mackenzie King, who served for a total of 21 years as Prime Minister while secretly engaged in spiritualist inquiry.
author: Robin

title: Episode 635: His Cousin Latte Dick
segment: Finally the Consulting Occultist looks at collector and art historian Aby Warburg.
author: Robin

title: Episode 630: Live at Dragonmeet 2024
segment: [No Consulting Occultist segment written up in this episode's show notes.]
author: Robin

title: Episode 629: Laugh With You Near Them
segment: Finally the Consulting Occultist raps tables and psychically excites the zither for a look at Victorian spiritualist William Stainton Moses.
author: Robin

title: Episode 626: She Did Say Independent
segment: And finally the Consulting Occultist profiles influential 19th century proto-feminist spiritualist Anna Kingsford.
author: Robin

title: Episode 622: In the Interest of Asking a Question
segment: Finally the Consulting Occultist surveys the works of early astrologer Gan De, who catalogued the stars during China’s Warring States period.
author: Robin

title: Episode 619: The Zeppo of the Golden Dawn
segment: Finally the Consulting Occultist delves into the career of coroner and Golden Dawn co-founder William Wynn Westcott.
author: Robin

title: Episode 615: Ranger Problems
segment: Finally the Consulting Occultist cracks the code on pioneering steganographer Johannes Trithemius.
author: Robin

title: Episode 611: Terrible Ideas Deflation
segment: Finally the Consulting Occultist lays out everything you need to know to placate a Mesopotamian ghost.
author: Robin

title: Episode 608: The Power is in the Booties
segment: Finally at the behest of silver-stringed backer Jon Biscoe, the Consulting Occultist looks at two magical tomes mentioned in Manly Wade Wellman’s Silver John Stories, the Grand Albert and the Long-Lost Friend.
author: Robin

title: Episode 604: It Takes a While to Work
segment: Finally the Consulting Occultist looks at 1920s celebrity astrologer Evangeline Adams, who doled out stock market tips, gave readings to moguls, movie stars and a monarch, and predicted her own death.
author: Robin

title: Episode 601: Hydrate and Fix Your Boot
segment: Finally, the Consulting Occultist, surprised to find that he has yet to cover Nostradamus after more than a decade of episodes, proceeds to do just that.
author: Robin

title: Episode 596: Also a No Show Job
segment: Finally the Consulting Occultist looks at the career of Icelandic medium Indriði Indriðason.
author: Robin

title: Episode 594: Bishop of the Drowned Land
segment: And finally the Consulting Occultist profiles Maria de Naglowska, occult lecturer and sex magician of 20s and 30s Paris.
author: Robin

title: Episode 591: Good Fun That Barbarians Have
segment: The Consulting Occultist wraps up the episode with the rundown on the 17th century English physician and mystic Robert Fludd.
author: Robin

title: Episode 583: Marge the Druid is Feeling Rejected
segment: Finally the Consulting Occultist meets us in the Food Hut at the behest of distinguished patron Elias Helfer, seconded by Jamie Twine, who seek the esoteric secrets of the pavlova.
author: Robin

title: Episode 580: The Most Fun Word in the History of Metallurgy
segment: Finally the Consulting Occultist tells the wry tale of the early Georgian era astrologer Isaac Bickerstaff, who predicted the death of rival John Partridge and might not have been entirely who he seemed.
author: Robin

title: Episode 575: Your Starter Past Life
segment: Finally the Consulting Occultist tells us about turn of the century Swiss medium, automatic writer and vicarious Mars visitor Hélène Smith.
author: Robin

title: Episode 572: Rains of Peas and Gravel
segment: Finally stellar backer Scott Wachter asks the Consulting Occultist why the so-called 13th astrological sign, Ophiuchus, remains unrecognized.
author: Robin

title: Episode 568: Dream Ghosts
segment: Finally the Consulting Occultist fills intrepid backer Jacob Boersma in on the alchemists, demonologists and kabbalists of his native Netherlands.
author: Robin

title: Episode 564: Speciation Event
segment: Finally the Consulting Occultist looks at self-styled shaman Durek Verrett, provider of woo-woo to the stars and fiancée of Norwegian princess Martha Louise.
author: Robin

title: Episode 562: Never Refer to Peoples’ Necks
segment: Finally the Consulting Occultist looks at the alchemical side of pioneering scientist and mathematician Isaac Newton.
author: Robin

title: Episode 558: Any Other Stupid Cave Lake
segment: And finally the Consulting Occultist regales us with tales true and folkloric concerning Ursula Southill, remembered by history as the witch and prophetess Mother Shipton.
author: Robin

title: Episode 555: Do We Get a Deal for Seven?
segment: Finally the Consulting Occultist tells the tale of a magical assassination plot aimed at Edward II.
author: Robin

title: Episode 552: Worse Hats
segment: Finally beloved Patreon backer Gerald Sears wants the Consulting Occultist to tell him what happened to occultism in America in the 40s and 50s.
author: Robin

title: Episode 547: Such Things as No
segment: Finally the Consulting Occultist meets us in the Architecture Hut for a look at the so-called Devil’s Architect, Nicholas Hawksmoor, whose reputation for the arcane might just have been burnished by some of our finest living nerdtropers.
author: Robin

title: Episode 542: Classic Snake Swap-Out
segment: Finally, the Consulting Occultist profiles 1st century prophet Alexander of Abonoteichus, begetter of the Glykon snake cult.
author: Robin

title: Episode 539: Players and/or Humans
segment: Then the Consulting Occultist enters the Culture Hut at the behest of sophisticated backer Jake Maas, who seeks enlightenment on the Theosophical roots of avant garde artist Yves Klein.
author: Robin

title: Episode 535: NASA is Not in the Neck Adjustment Business
segment: Finally, at the behest of inquiring backer Sam Rutzick, the Consulting Occultist warily goes undercover for some questionable spinal adjustment, in search of the ghostly weirdness behind chiropractic treatment.
author: Robin

title: Episode 534: Manimal Utopia
segment: [No Consulting Occultist segment written up in this episode's show notes.]
author: Robin

title: Episode 531: The Copts Still Have His Head
segment: Finally the Consulting Occultist meets erudite backer Jamie Twine in a gondola for a survey of the weird and magical in Venice.
author: Robin

title: Episode 526: From What Steve Did
segment: Finally, at the behest of insightful backer Benjamin Rawls, the Consulting Occultist turns his gaze to Japan and its Happy Science cult.
author: Robin

title: Episode 523: Never Seen With a Windsock
segment: Finally the Consulting Occultist reaches into his tarot collection for HEXEN 2.0, making this also a Culture Hut installment about its creator, contemporary artist Suzanne Treister.
author: Robin

title: Episode 518: A Big Fan of Heliocentrism
segment: Finally at the request of beloved Patreon backer Louis Sylvester, the Consulting Occultist profiles Cotton Mather.
author: Robin

title: Episode 514: Joseph Campbell in a Jar
segment: Finally forward-thinking backer Tennant Reed visits the Consulting Occultist for the esoteric motivation behind repeated thefts of the preserved head of reformer and utilitarian philosopher Jeremy Bentham.
author: Robin

title: Episode 511: I Just Wanted to Say Astrosome
segment: Finally the Consulting Occultist lets love into his heart for a profile of songwriter, polemicist and free love priestess Edith Maida Lessing.
author: Robin

title: Episode 506: His Bonaparte Eyes
segment: Finally estimable backer John Kingdon asks the Consulting Occultist to profile Gerald Gardner, fashioner of modern Wicca.
author: Robin

title: Episode 502: Aramaic Mutterer Whisperer
segment: Finally at the behest of tart and tannic backers Fridrik Bjarnason and Tim Vert, the Consulting Occultist joins us in the Food Hut to survey the esoteric side of wine.
author: Robin

title: Episode 494: Sidekicks to the Bear
segment: And back to the recreation, the Consulting Occultist provides a profile of 13th century magician Roger Bacon.
author: Robin

title: Episode 490: They Left the Irony Bridge Intact
segment: Finally the Consulting Occultist examines the esoteric influence on, and of, Swami Vivekananda, founder of yoga as we know it.
author: Robin

title: Episode 487: Nothing More Organic Than a Moon
segment: Finally sagacious backer Jeff Greenlee visits the Consulting Occultist for the lowdown on the surprisingly steamy story of the Jesus’ Wife hoax.
author: Robin

title: Episode 482: I Never Really Got Rid of Her
segment: Finally estimable Patreon backer Robert Wolfe asks the Consulting Occultist about Mary Poppins creator P. L. Travers and her association with mystical philosopher George Gurdjieff.

title: Episode 478: His Pursevant Had Dropsy
segment: Finally estimable backer Michael David Jr. enjoins the Consulting Occultist to profile English writer and Catholic decadent Montague Summers.
author: Robin

title: Episode 473: The Special Ability is It’s a Gun
segment: Continuing with the Western theme, estimable backer Ian Carlsen visits the Consulting Occultist to learn the esoteric significance of Wild Bill Hickok’s Dead Man’s Hand.

title: Episode 468: Weird Mushroom Things and a Swirl Over There
segment: Finally estimable Patreon backer P. O’Neil seeks the Consulting Occultist’s files on a Theosophist retirement community in Ojai, California.
author: Robin

title: Episode 463: The Bubble Wrap of Roleplaying Games
segment: Finally the Consulting Occultist profiles the mystical psychologist Claudio Naranjo.
author: Robin

title: Episode 458: Slacker Infant
segment: Finally the Consulting Occultist confronts a head-scratcher only one of his particular esoteric set of skills can solve: why is there a floating baby in a clamshell in the Chicago seal?
author: Robin

title: Episode 455: Ambush Us With Ghosts
segment: Then numerology takes on a TikTok twist as the Consulting Occultist examines Grabovoi numbers.
author: Robin

title: Episode 449: Quietly Judging You
segment: Finally the Consulting Occultist profiles a genuine arch-villain of the occult, the Rasputin or Argentina, José López Rega.
author: Robin

title: Episode 446: Well Also H. R. Giger
segment: Finally, shadowy Patreon backer Dave from Washington enters the parlor of the Consulting Occultist hoping he may shed light on the appearance of Theosophical symbols on the control console in Ridley Scott’s Alien.
author: Robin

title: Episode 442: Mad at Unicorns
segment: Finally the Consulting Occultist again shows you his picture collection as an erudite but anonymous Patreon backer asks for the lowdown on Art Nouveau illustrator and occult theorist Austin Osman Spare.
author: Robin

title: Episode 438: Anyone Can Bilocate
segment: Finally estimable Patreon backer Ed Sizemore checks in with the Consulting Occultist for the story of Baird Spalding and Douglas DeVorss, which starts with an ancient book of desert secrets.
author: Robin

title: Episode 435: Unreliable Satanists
segment: Finally enigmatic Patreon backer Bt taps on the door of the Consulting Occultist for the supernatural secrets of automaton maker John Joseph Merlin.
author: Robin

title: Episode 431: Working for Bigfoot
segment: Finally the Holy Roman Empire once again rears its head as perceptive Patreon backer Paul Douglas visits the Consulting Occultist to learn about Michael Scot, court magician to Frederick II.
author: Robin

title: Episode 428: Only a Modernist Horse
segment: Finally the Consulting Occultist dons his paisley smoking jacket for an inquiry into the occultism of LSD advocate Timothy Leary.
author: Robin

title: Episode 425: Pork Chop of the Truth
segment: Finally the Consulting Occultist reviews the case files from the 1968 robbery of Israel Regardie’s Los Angeles library.
author: Robin

title: Episode 422: Sumerian GM Screens
segment: Finally, the Consulting Occultist for the first time trains his eye on a subject who is both a paranormalist and an RPG designer, James “Herbie” Brennan.
author: Robin

title: Episode 419: Five Hour Haste
segment: Finally the Consulting Occultist goes literary as Thomas Casey seeks info on the supernatural leanings of Portuguese modernist writer Fernando Pessoa.
author: Robin

title: Episode 416: They’re Gonna Have Their Lifeforms Fight
segment: Finally Elias Helfer trudges with us up the cobwebbed stairs to the parlor of the Consulting Occultist for a look at iconic Tarot deck illustrator Pamela Colman Smith.
author: Robin

title: Episode 411: Chief Plant Health Officer
segment: Finally the Consulting Occultist brandishes his planchette to explain the Ouija board and its inventor, Elijah Bond.
author: Robin

title: Episode 408: Not That My Players Would Do Anything Like That
segment: Reflective Patreon backer Corey Pierno seeks the Consulting Occultist’s wisdom on crystals and their history as supernatural objects.
author: Robin

title: Episode 404: Toss In Your Werecows
segment: Finally, admirable Patreon backer John W S Marvin calls on the untapped military knowledge of the Consulting Occultist for a look at the esoteric secrets behind the Battle of Kursk.
author: Robin

title: Episode 398: Dragons Love Merch
segment: Finally estimable Patreon backer Fred Kiesche heads into the Edwardian parlor of the Consulting Occultist for a profile of Russian painter, archaeologist and theosophist Nicolas Roerich.
author: Robin

title: Episode 394: Fruit Cutting Policies
segment: Finally the Consulting Occultist elucidates the effort to market famously ill-reviewed 50s fantasy novel Kinsmen of the Dragon as an esoteric tome.
author: Robin

title: Episode 390: Open a Can Hut
segment: Finally esteemed Patreon backer Peter Williamson visits the Consulting Occultist to learn about economist, mystic, poet and painter George Russell.
author: Robin

title: Episode 385: Shill for the Macedonians
segment: Then we conclude our Consulting Occultist series on key figures of the magical Belle Epoque with dynamic duo Oswald Wirth and  Stanislas de Guaita.
author: Robin

title: Episode 384: Number One Nightmare
segment: The Consulting Occultist continues our series of arcane profiles from Belle Epoque Paris with a look at Ely Star, a protege of Robert Houdin who infused his astrology with kabbalism.
author: Robin

title: Episode 383: Not Quite Doctor Cowboy
segment: The Consulting Occultist continues to wear suspiciously Robin-like facial hair as we enter the penultimate edition of Belle Epoque magicians series, as inspired by The Yellow King Roleplaying Game, with the scene’s fun couple,  Moina and Samuel Liddell Mathers, of Golden Dawn fame.
author: Robin

title: Episode 382: Health and Safety
segment: Then the Consulting Occultist continues our series on the magicians of the Parisian Belle Epoque, as we drop into the shop of bookseller, composer and occult publisher Edmond Bailly.
author: Robin

title: Episode 381: Anthropodermic Wallet
segment: Finally the Consulting Occultist resumes our interrupted look at the 1890s Parisian supernatural scene with a profile of the Martinist writer and salon organizer Papus.
author: Robin

title: Episode 380: He Said, Foreshadowingly
segment: Finally the Consulting Occultist returns to matters Rosicrucian as beloved backer, the Monster Talk podcast, asks for secrets of Christian Rosenkreuz’s legendary tomb.
author: Robin

title: Episode 373: Nutty Crab Soup [Corrected]
segment: Finally the Consulting Occultist answers a request from beloved Patreon backers Tim Maness and Jean Bauer for the lowdown on the 1911 Ark of the Covenant expedition.
author: Robin

title: Episode 369: Lotta Garlic There Though
segment: Then the Consulting Occultist acquaints us with a central figure in the field of magical speculation, Cornelius Agrippa.
author: Robin

title: Episode 367: Ungrateful Mopes
segment: Finally the Consulting Occultist heads to balmy British Columbia to tell the tale of yellow-robed cultist Brother XII.
author: Robin

title: Episode 360: Sir Gawain “the Rock” Johnson
segment: Finally the Consulting Occultist takes a day trip to the Tower of London to inspect the arcane sigils of imprisoned sorcerer Hew Draper. Want to pose a question to the show? Get your priority question asking access with your support for the KARTAS Patreon!
author: Robin

title: Episode 355: If Abstract Expressionism Doesn’t Do the Trick
segment: The Consulting Occultist doesn’t usually muss his smoking jacket in the realm of politics, but with Marianne Williamson running for President he has little choice but to roll up his sleeves.
author: Robin

title: Episode 352: We’ve Still Got that Other Folder
segment: Then the Consulting Occultist calls for order in the court as we hear the confession of a Livonian werewolf.
author: Robin

title: Episode 349: Bad Odds in a Balloon
segment:
In the Gaming Hut Patreon backer Ethan Schoonover asks us to explain the bespoke game terms we use, from F20 to GMC.

Mikko Airaksinen puts a chill in the History Hut with his request for the esoteric scoop behind the Swedish North Pole Expedition of 1897.

Our epic spate of Ken and Robin Recycle Audio excerpts from Carcosa Con concludes with a look at Robin’s build-out of the Chambers mythos into The Yellow King Roleplaying Game.

Finally backer Chris Kalley, seconded by Jay Tea, asks the Consulting Occultist to blow the lid off magical Buenos Aires.

Want to pose a question to the show? Get your priority question asking access with your support for the KARTAS Patreon!



Snag Ken and Robin merchandise at TeePublic.







Over the Edge, the twisted game of counter-culture conspiracy, returns in a completely remagined edition by its original creator, Jonathan Tweet. Grab it wherever fine Atlas Games are sold, on June 1st.





Ken’s latest roleplaying game, The Fall of Delta Green, is now available in print or PDF or both from Pelgrane Press. Journey to the head-spinning chaos of the late 1960s, back when everyone’s favorite anti-Cthulhu special ops agency hadn’t gone rogue yet, for this pulse-pounding GUMSHOE game of war, covert action, and Mythos horror.





The treasures of Askfageln can be found at DriveThruRPG. Get all issues of FENIX since 2013 available in special English editions. Score metric oodles of Ken Hite gaming goodness, along with equally stellar pieces by Graeme Davis and Pete Nash. Warning: in English, not in Swedish. In English, not Swedish. While you’re at it, grab DICE and Freeway Warrior!





Arc Dream Publishing presents a gorgeous new edition of Robert W. Chambers’ The King in Yellow, a deluxe hardback in delightful faux snakeskin, with a foreword by John Scott Tynes, annotations by our own Kenneth Hite, and stunning full-pate color  illustrations by Samuel Araya. Grab it while it lasts in the Arc Dream store.





https://kenandrobin.s3.us-west-2.amazonaws.com/media/Ken-And-Robin-Talk-About-Stuff-Episode-349.mp3
author: Robin

title: Episode 341: Trouser Leg of the Mythos
segment: Finally, at the behest of backer Andrea Colletta, we pop into the parlor of the Consulting Occultist to whip up the new sorcerous discipline of etymomancy.
author: Robin

title: Episode 335: Any Smart Gundamologist
segment: Finally backer Chris Kalley visits the Consulting Occultist to learn about military theorist, Thelemite and fascist J. F. C. Fuller.
author: Robin

title: Episode 332: Unencumbered By Space Cats
segment: Then backer Gerald Sears asks the Consulting Occultist to fill a game set in late 20s New York City with historical esoteric weirdness.
author: Robin

title: Episode 329: The Looming Party
segment: Finally, at the behest of backer Wayne Rossi, we turn to the Consulting Occultist for the mystical skinny on Carlos Castaneda.
author: Robin

title: Episode 326: Credulous Ghost-Hunting Dupe
segment: Finally the Consulting Occultist unwinds the deeply complicated tale of the Cock Lane Ghost, as first penetrated by intrepid occult investigator Samuel Johnson.
author: Robin

title: Episode 321: Red Dawn Times
segment: The other Consulting Occultist continues our Yellow King-inspired series on occultists of the Belle Epoque with a look at the original neo-Gnostic, Jules Doinel.
author: Robin

title: Episode 320: Ghost Rationing Policy
segment: Our survey of Belle Epoque weirdness ducks into the Eliptony Hut with a profile of early parapsychologist Albert de Rochas.
author: Robin

title: Episode 319: Eventually You Run Out of Zigs
segment: Finally the Consulting Occultist changes identities to embark on a series on the wizards and paranormalists you might encounter in 1890s Paris. First up: pioneering trickster Leo Taxil.
author: Robin

title: Episode 315: Eloi au Vin
segment: Finally it’s time to stay positive as backer Dan Kassiday asks the Consulting Occultist to discuss the nocebo effect explanation for curses.
author: Robin

title: Episode 310: The Social Media Impact of Ulthar
segment: Finally backer Derek Upham visits the Consulting Occultist for a look at artist James Bridle, who has constructed a magic circle to trap self-driving cars.
author: Robin

title: Episode 307: Nobody Wants to Be a Gate
segment: Finally backer Hal Mangold asks the Consulting Occultist to review the career of Australian artist and Pagan sex magician Rosaleen Norton.
author: Robin

title: Episode 304: We’re Not Running a Party of Death Here
segment: Finally the Consulting Occultist profiles Alexander Dugin, a Russian philosopher who combines fascism, synarchy, nationalism and chaos magick under the banner of an oddly familiar eight-pointed arrow star.
author: Robin

title: Episode 301: Thanks For Knowing We Were Right
segment: If someone tells you their favorite esoteric writer is Julius Evola, you just heard your cue to back away slowly. The Consulting Occultist is here to tell you why.
author: Robin

title: Episode 298: Now We’re Just Gygaxing
segment: Finally the Consulting Occultist opens a perilous tome to tell us about Reginald Scot and The Discoverie of Witchcraft.
author: Robin

title: Episode 295: That’s Why They Call It Ichor
segment: And Ash Jackson is the Scrollbard meets us in the parlor of the Consulting Occultist to get each of us to circumscribe a sacred alphabet.
author: Robin

title: Episode 291: Where Magic Goes to Die
segment: Then the Consulting Occultist goes West Coast as we examine the ley line implications of the L.A. Times leaving its iconic art deco headquarters.
author: Robin

title: Episode 290: Leaves Don’t Work on PayPal
segment: Finally, hold onto your library cards as, thanks to a query from backer Michael Parker, the Consulting Occultist investigates the theft of an Isaac Newton manuscript from the Carnegie Rare Books room.
author: Robin

title: Episode 284: There’s Always a Worse Writer Death
segment: Finally the Consulting Occultist finds another Nazi mystic to talk about, as Patreon backer Bret Kramer asks for a profile on Savitri Devi.
author: Robin

title: Episode 282: Haunted Anarchist Texts
segment: Recovering from the red carpet, we visit the Consulting Occultist, who has been checking out the initial offerings placed online by the Ritman Library of occult texts.
author: Robin

title: Episode 279: Logical Analogy Shock
segment: Then the Consulting Occultist admits backer Hoyle Anderson to his Edwardian snuggery for a cuppa and discussion of zodiac cup tasseography.
author: Robin

title: Episode 274: What Would Thagdar Do?
segment: Finally, backer Tim Vert checks in with the Consulting Occultist on the status of a possible eclipse child.
author: Robin

title: Episode 270: Nobody Wants to be a Waistcoat
segment: And finally the Consulting Occultist fulfills the request of Patreon backer Paul by filling us in on the witches of Chiloe.

title: Episode 267: The First Two Things are Metaphors For the Third
segment: We end on a Gallic switcheroo as Robin dons the mantle of Consulting Occultist to fulfill a request from Patreon backer Paul to talk about the Rosicrucian magician Josephin Peladan. Watch for his appearance in The Yellow King Roleplaying Game.

title: Episode 263: All Seven Spleens
segment: Then, following a prophecy laid down to us by Patreon backer Rafael Pabst, the Consulting Occultist looks at the career of poet and illustrator William Blake, with a special focus on the visionary, angel-seeing part.

title: Episode 259: All the Cute Girls are Capulets
segment: Finally the Consulting Occultist serves up a reply to Patreon backer David Shaw, who seeks the secret truth on occult Kashmir.

title: [Repost] Episode 255: The Devil’s Condiment
segment: Finally the Consulting Occultist goes inside, uh Posadism, as backer Stewart Robertson asks about Posadism.

title: Episode 255: The Devil’s Condiment
segment: Finally the Consulting Occultist goes inside, uh Posadism, as backer Stewart Robertson asks about Posadism.

title: Episode 253: That’s More of a Gloss on Aristotle
segment: And the Consulting Occultist gets all semiotic to reply to a query from Patreon backer Polydamas on what the Secret Masters were up to when they invented post-modernism.

title: Episode 247: So Fair It Shines as a Marketing Quote
segment: Finally Patreon backer Tim Vert knows who he’s gonna call. He asks the Consulting Occultist to tell us all about Harry Houdini’s ghostbusters.
author: Robin

title: Episode 244: You Have to Sit in the Jar
segment: Finally the Consulting Occultist looks at the arcane geography of 1930s Los Angeles times as manipulated by L.A. Times publisher Harry Chandler and revealed in the pages of Cthulhu Confidential.
author: Robin

title: Episode 243: I Do Not Wish to Be a Moth Hat
segment: And when Ken was in Berlin getting his fangs fitted, he took time out to visit a museum. Hence, the Consulting Occultist recounts the wonders of the alchemy exhibit at the Kulturforum.
author: Robin

title: Episode 240: Bush Bachelor
segment: Then get ready to grab your picket signs as our Tell Me More segment enlists the Consulting Occultist for a look at the secret occult history of the epic battle between urban planner Robert Moses and the writer Jane Jacobs over the fate of Greenwich Village.
author: Robin

title: Episode 238: The Titular Goat in This Reenactment
segment: Finally we check in on the Consulting Occultist as backer Wayne Rossi demands the occult truth behind the Elvis-Nixon meeting.
author: Robin

title: Episode 235: He-Man’s Woman Hating Civilization
segment: And in a switcheroo, Robin dons the smoking jacket of the Consulting Occultist as we look at the cultists and gurus of 1930s L.A., as seen in Cthulhu Confidential.
author: Robin

title: Episode 231: Please Compensate For Our Brutal Incompetence
segment: Then backer Jacob Ansari meets the Consulting Occultist at the baggage carousel to ask about the sorcerous significance of the Denver International Airport.
author: Robin

title: Episode 227: Other Buopoths Do
segment: Then the Consulting Occultist unfurls some alchemical wisdom on a Paul Tevis request for the skinny on the Ripley Scrolls.
author: Robin

title: Episode 225: Just Make Up a Bunch of Dwarves
segment: Finally we enlist the Consulting Occultist to reveal the esoteric weirdness lurking in your bowl of Chex cereal, as he tells all about Ralstonism.
author: Robin

title: Episode 223: There’s No Blood in a JPEG
segment: Finally the Consulting Occultist, somewhat to his surprise, realizes he has yet to tell us about Eliphas Levi, and so tells us about Eliphas Levi.
author: Robin

title: Episode 219: Argue With Ceramics
segment: Keep a careful watch as the Consulting Occultist whispers of Les Veilleurs, an Parisian occult group founded in the final days of WWI.
author: Robin

title: Episode 213: I Would Have Been On the Other Side of That
segment: We conclude with the Consulting Occultist talking about Jakob Böhme, trying to convince Robin that there is something of interest to be gleaned from yet another Christian mystic.
author: Robin

title: Episode 203: Pull the Damn Trigger You Coward!
segment: Thanks to Patreon backer Morgan Ellis, we have a Consulting Occultist topic Ken can field in his sleep and/or the day before a big show: ley lines.
author: Robin

title: Episode 202: While We’re On the Surface of the Freezing Sun
segment: Karl Schmidt wants the Consulting Occultist to spill the astrological and alchemical beans on Rudolf II of the Holy Roman Empire.
author: Robin

title: Episode 199: Not That That’s What Time Travelers Say
segment: Then we’re back in the parlor of the Consulting Occultist to hear the tale of reputed 16th century sorcerer Rinaldo des Trois-Echelles du Mayne.
author: Robin

title: Episode 195: All Its Guns in Norman Rockwell
segment: And hey, it’s another all request episode, as backer James Griffin quizes the Consulting Occultist on past life regression. Why isn’t it a thing any more?
author: Robin

title: Episode 192: Emulating a Genre That Doesn’t Exist
segment: Finally the Consulting Occultist satisfies Patreon backer Allen Wilkins’ desire to hear about writer Alan Moore’s occult thought.
author: Robin

title: Episode 189: It Won’t Fix Me But It Will Fix That
segment: Finally backer Joshua Hillerup climbs the creaky stairs that lead to the Consulting Occultist to learn about the occult symbolism of flowers.
author: Robin

title: Episode 186: You Need Canapes To Have a Secret Society
segment: Finally the Consulting Occultist slakes the hankering of Patreon backer Stewart Robertson for info on the Black Dragon Society.
author: Robin

title: Episode 181: Taking Latitudes with Longitude
segment: Then the Consulting Occultist tours us through the colorful career of privateer, courtier, cookbook author and magic powder maker Kenelm Digby. The connection? The rejected magical dog injury method of longitude measurement.
author: Robin

title: Episode 177: You’ve Got Your Chainsaw Working
segment: Finally, the Consulting Occultist fills us in on Paracelsus, a literal Renaissance man whose achievements in medicine, psychology and toxicology also included influential works of a Hermetic bent.
author: Robin

title: Episode 175: And Then Everyone Gets Indicted
segment: Then in the most momentous crossover team up event since Superman faced off against Ali, the Consulting Occulist enters the Tradecraft Hut to recount the fascinating career of outsiders’ insider Tom Driberg. Only a certain percentage of the legends surrounding him turn out to be too good to check.
author: Robin

title: Episode 172: #notAllAstrologers
segment: While in London in December, the Consulting Occultist got a sneak peek at key items of John Dee’s book collection. Join us in previewing the upcoming exhibit Scholar, courtier, magician: the lost library of John Dee, which runs Jan 18-July 29  at the Royal College of Physicians.
author: Robin

title: Episode 168: Real Made-Up History
segment: Finally the Consulting Occultist gets architectural to discuss the occult tendencies of Le Corbusier.
author: Robin

title: Episode 164: Another Excuse To Drink In the Street
segment: Finally the Consulting Occultist delves into the North Berwick witch trials, in which opponents of King James VI / I found themselves on the wrong end of a Halloween-related accusation.
author: Robin

title: Episode 157: I Have Points in Both, Simon
segment: Oft-mentioned but not until now explicated, the Priory of Sion finally gets the full treatment from the Consulting Occultist.
author: Robin

title: Episode 154: Orange and Tufty From the Primordial Swamp
segment: Then the Consulting Occultist risks possible heckling from a brazen head as he looks at the life and legend of Roger Bacon.
author: Robin

title: Episode 149: Contemptible People Tavern
segment: Finally we bathe ourselves in light as the Consulting Occultist profiles the preeminent practitioner of nice magic, Dion Fortune.
author: Robin

title: Episode 147: The Worst Doppelganger
segment: Impose your will on reality through whatever lens you want as the Consulting Occultist casts a gimlet eye on chaos magick.
author: Robin

title: Episode 144: Chaotic Prolix
segment: Then the Consulting Occultist gives us the lowdown on alchemist, mystic and eponymous library founder Elias Ashmole.
author: Robin

title: Episode 137: Clown Moistening Skills
segment: Finally the Consulting Occultist answers a request from Bryant Durrell for the 101 on spiritualist polymath Rudolf Steiner.
author: Robin

title: Episode 135: So Bad the Quakers Riot
segment: Then the Consulting Occultist builds electric Jesus in its barn as we look at the career of John Murray Spear.
author: Robin

title: Episode 133: Wielding Fennel Without Proper Church Authority
segment: Finally the Consulting Occultist introduces us to the Italian benandanti, who as late as the 16th century were entering dreams to battle witches and pirates.
author: Robin

title: Episode 131: Helping People Steal Concrete
segment: Finally the Consulting Occultist crosses over with Among My Many Hats as Ken tells us about his Goetia instalment of Ken Writes About Stuff.
author: Robin

title: Episode 126: Rhode Island Jones
segment: Finally, in response to a Nick Eden request, the Consulting Occultist introduces us to that seeming contradiction in terms, the Soviet Occult.
author: Robin

title: Episode 119: Sex Argentina
segment: Our Dreamhounds of Paris-inspired visits to the Consulting Occultist continue with a consideration of mystical seeker George Gurdjieff.
author: Robin

title: Episode 118: There’s Nothing In It For Owls
segment: The Consulting Occultist continues his Parisian sojourn with an examination of uxurious bookseller Nicolas Flamel, to whom later rumor and Harry Potter imputed alchemical mastery.
author: Robin

title: Episode 117: Covered in Cats and Croissant Crumbs
segment: Back to matters Gallic, the Consulting Occultist details the life, thought, and possible Dreamlands influence of fin de siècle writer, ritualist and schmoozer Gerard Encausse.
author: Robin

title: Episode 116: Okay, I’ll Go Get Your Leg
segment: Blasting his trumpet for Dreamhounds of Paris, the Consulting Occultist begins a mini-arc delving into the French occult, starting with scientist, spiritualist and polymath Charles Richet.
author: Robin

title: Episode 115: Anopheles Mosquito, Stagnant Pond
segment: Finally the Consulting Occultist makes an appearance in the Tradecraft Hut to discuss revelations that British psy-ops thought it would calm the Northern Irish conflict if they staged evidence of black masses.
author: Robin

title: Episode 112: Someone Will Break Out Into Recipes
segment: Finally, the Consulting Occultist looks at the witch-related thoughts of once-influential scholar Margaret Murray.
author: Robin

title: Episode 109: Put a Pointy Wizard Hat On It
segment: Finally the Consulting Occultist gives us the real lowdown on synarchy.
author: Robin

title: Episode 105: Does This Look Like a Railroad to You?
segment: Finally, Sarah Helen Whitman, spiritualist and jilted fiancee of Edgar Allen Poe, summons herself into a particularly gossipy installment of Consulting Occultist.
author: Robin

title: Episode 102: Nonsense is on the March
segment: Thanks to a reminder from listener Joe Taylor, we finally let the Consulting Occultist loose on the Cathars, as we’ve been planning to do all along.
author: Robin

title: Episode 99: Methane Planet of the Rakshasas
segment: Then the Consulting Occultist goes psychedelic with a look at the Source Family and its karate-chopping, bank robbing New Age guru, Father Yod.
author: Robin

title: Episode 97: Hamsterface
segment: We close with the Consulting Occultist, who tells us of the London stone and the magical significance of its current ignominious location.
author: Robin

title: Episode 94: The Simpering Bridegroom
segment: Finally the Consulting Occultist looks at husband and wife demon-busters Ed and Lorraine Warren.
author: Robin

title: Episode 92: The Werepig Demographic
segment: Finally, Ken, having tackled the topic in a two-part Ken Writes About Stuff entry, dons the Consulting Occultist hat to introduce us to the basics of voodoo.
author: Robin

title: Episode 89: The Elvis of the European Occult Underground
segment: The Consulting Occultist finishes his major figures of the French enlightenment trilogy with the immortal Comte de Saint Germain.
author: Robin

title: Episode 87: The Whacking Shovel
segment: Finally the Consulting Occultist pulls a long con and at least half of a badger game as we consider the grifting career of Cagliostro, alchemist and influential Freemason.
author: Robin

title: Episode 84: Those Punk Magicians Killed My Elephant
segment: Get sleepy, very sleepy, letting go of cares and worries as the Consulting Occultist casts your mind toward the tale of Franz Anton Mesmer.
author: Robin

title: Episode 81: If I Were an Otter
segment: Consulting Occultist meets Tradecraft Hut as, at the request of listener Johnstone Metzger, we examine the work and shocking demise of mythological studies scholar and Romanian dissident Ioan Culianu.
author: Robin

title: Episode 79: Take That, Entertainment
segment: Responding to popular demand, the Consulting Occultist assesses competing new claims of having cracked the cryptobotanical mysteries of the Voynich Manuscript.
author: Robin

title: Episode 76: Hunker in the Bunker
segment: Then Ken dons his Consulting Occultist regalia to tear the lid off the djinn menace.
author: Robin

title: Episode 73: Guff About Other Planets
segment: The Consulting Occultist returns to the spiritualist movement of the 19th century to contemplate the Swedenborg-cribbing visions of Andrew Jackson Davis, the Poughkeepsie Seer.
author: Robin

title: Episode 68: Why You Shouldn’t Ask Us to Destroy Civilization
segment: Then Ask Ken and Robin visits the Consulting Occultist on behalf of Steven W. King, who wants to know what to put on his beginning occult bookshelf.
author: Robin

title: Episode 65: Cherubs Who Have Aged Out
segment: Finally Ken uses his high-level Consulting Occultist borrowing privileges to scrutinize the legendary Voynich Manuscript.
author: Robin

title: Episode 62: Watch Out, Boys, He Has the Power of Transcendentalism
segment: Finally, with a sigh of palpable reluctance, the Consulting Occultist educates us on the Transcendentalism of Ralph Waldo Emerson.
author: Robin

title: Episode 60: Problems With Sabertooth Tigers
segment: Finally the Consulting Occultist goes back to basics to study the surprisingly young history of the Tarot.
author: Robin

title: Episode 56: Oh, My Pancreas
segment: Finally we give the weary Consulting Occultist a topic he should be able to do while standing on his head—those diffusely mystical purveyors of alchemical imagery, the Rosicrucians.
author: Robin

title: Episode 52: The Delightful Burning Sensation You Alluded To Earlier
segment: Then look up to the strangest sight in the skyline of Bandung, Indonesia as, at the request of Tom McGrenery, the Consulting Occultist tries to infuse its Octopus House with more weirdness than it already contains.
author: Robin

title: Episode 48: Except for All the Cannibalism
segment: Then the Consulting Occultist completes his obligations as a guide to the Nazi occult with a look at the scholarship-distorting, expedition launching academic department of the SS, the Ahnenerbe.
author: Robin

title: Episode 47: An Occasional Underused Wendigo
segment: Then our epic Consulting Occultist series on the Nazi Occult explores the Thule Society, wealthy right-wing imaginers of lost continents who built an anti-Communist goon squad only to see it morph into the Nazi party.
author: Robin

title: Episode 46: An Indictment of Your Pudding
segment: Then we head over to chat with the Consulting Occultist, as part four of our series on Nazi occultism leads us to metaphorically heft the Spear of Destiny.
author: Robin

title: Episode 45: A Blatant Vowel Swap
segment: We wrap with part 3 of our Consulting Occultist series on the Nazi occult, this time focusing in on Irminist prophet and Himmler advisor Karl-Maria Wiligut.
author: Robin

title: Episode 44: The Marshy Aunt
segment: Finally, part two of our epic Consulting Occultist series on the Nazi occult sets the stage with Volkisch movements, Guido von List, and the cast of variously psychopathic characters who bring occult thought with them into Hitler’s high command.
author: Robin

title: Episode 39: The Book of Not At All Sinister Church Names
segment: We close with a visit to the Consulting Occultist, who recounts the Theosophy-meets-Evangelical-meets-New Age tale of Elizabeth Clare Prophet.
author: Robin

title: Episode 37: Ghost-Kissed Pilsner
segment: Then we move on to more entertaining and distant history, as, at the request of Ask Lovecraft, the Consulting Occultist tells us tales of Paschal Beverly Randolph, 19th century exponent of Mesmerism, pre-Adamism, and wholesome sex magic.
author: Robin

title: Episode 32: Cash in Your Magic Swords
segment: Finally we meet the Consulting Occultist in the non-standard confines of the Tradecraft Hut, where we open an Elizabethan dossier on spy and playwright Christopher Marlowe.
author: Robin

title: Episode 30: Ring-Tailed and Fructivorous
segment: And finally, with such matters back in the news, we tap the Consulting Occultist for a 101 class on lost continents, from Atlantis to Hyperborea and beyond.
author: Robin

title: Episode 27: League of Extraordinary Skeletons
segment: Finally, in Consulting Occultist, we review the stream-crossing career of African-American magician, escape artist and hoodoo worker Black Herman.
author: Robin

title: Episode 25: Finally, the Woodrow Wilson Throwdown
segment: Finally the Consulting Occultist considers the silver-shirted biography of American screenwriter, vision-haver and fascist William Dudley Pelley.
author: Robin

title: Episode 22: Her Agile, Ventriloquistic Foot
segment: We wrap with an ironic tale for the Consulting Occultist—the sad story of Margaret Fox, who accidentally founded 19th century spiritualism through a teenage prank.
author: Robin

title: Episode 20: Twentieth Century Eccentrics
segment: We close with another visit to the Consulting Occultist, who this time around delves into the murky facts behind a reputed 17th century French Satanism outbreak, better known as the Affair of the Poisons.
author: Robin

title: Episode 15: A Mere Frustum
segment: Then Ken in his guise as the Consulting Occultist finds the standards-loving organist behind media-seeking Satanist Anton LaVey.
author: Robin

title: Episode 13: That Time We Burned Down the White House
segment: Finally we drop in again on the Consulting Occultist for an introduction to Kenneth Grant, whose role as Derleth to Aleister Crowley’s Lovecraft went as far as incorporating Shub-Niggurath into his ritual practices.
author: Robin

title: Episode 11: When Angels Tell You To Wife-Swap
segment: We close with the ever-popular Consulting Occult as Ken gives us the 101 on Elizabethan magician, mathematician and cartographer (sense a theme here?) John Dee.
author: Robin

title: Episode 8: Cruller-Infested Demi-Paradise
segment: Finally, we call again upon the Consulting Occultist for a primer on Aleister Crowley.
author: Robin

title: Episode 6: The Mystic Power of Indexing
segment: Finally we call upon Ken in his capacity as Consulting Occultist to introduce us to H. P. Blavatsky and the Theosophists.
author: Robin

title: Episode 3: The Audacity of Cupcakes
segment: Lastly but not leastly, Ken again dons the mantle of Consulting Occultist to examine the difference between a mystic and an occultist, as seen through the examples of Johnny Appleseed and Emannuel Swedenborg. Also referenced: Daniel Burnham, and Mitch Horowitz’s book Occult America.
author: Robin

title: Episode 1: Vampires are A-holes
segment: To round out the episode, Ken dons the mantle of Consulting Occultist to give us the 101 on The Hermetic Order of the Golden Dawn, with a cast of characters including William Butler Yeats, Bram Stoker, and Aleister Crowley.
author: Robin
